title: cremanthodium daochengense
intro: Cremanthodium daochengense, also known as Daocheng golden clover, is a rare and endangered perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of China. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Cremanthodium daochengense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts for medicinal use, although its availability and usage are largely limited due to its rarity and conservation status.
